home.heroIntro: Discover the inspiring new exhibit exploring the immigrant cultures that shaped the heart and soul of Pueblo.
home.welcomeTitle: Discover the people, places, and legacy of steelmaking.
home.welcomeText: This opening version is designed as a clean, expandable kiosk experience for one exhibit, with room to grow over time.

exhibit.title: A Long Journey To a New Land
exhibit.intro: This exhibit invites visitors to explore the inspiring stories and immigrant cultures that shaped the heart and soul of Pueblo.
exhibit.note: Replace this filler content with final exhibit title, intro copy, and the first set of approved stories or object highlights.

cards.title: Origins
cards.text: Early industrial growth, context, and significance.

cards.title: Workers & Families
cards.text: Community life, labor, and intergenerational impact.

cards.title: Artifacts & Objects
cards.text: A place for featured pieces, labels, and captions.

stories.title: Origins
paragraphs: Industrial growth in Pueblo connected labor, transportation, materials, and ambition in ways that reshaped both the region and the people living in it. This opening story can introduce visitors to that broader context.
In the final version, this page can pair archival photography with concise storytelling, a strong quote, and optional buttons for related content.
Keep the first launch lean. A kiosk works best when visitors can understand each section quickly and choose to go deeper only when they want to.

stories.title: Workers & Families
paragraphs: The story of steel is also the story of neighborhoods, families, routines, sacrifice, and pride. This section gives the kiosk emotional weight and helps visitors connect with lived experience.
If you receive even one strong oral history clip or personal quote, this page can become a real anchor point for the experience.
This is also a strong place for intergenerational photographs, school connections, immigration stories, or worker memory excerpts.

stories.title: Artifacts & Objects
paragraphs: Artifacts give visitors a tangible way to connect with industrial history. A few well-presented objects can do more than a long block of text.
For the event version, use a photo, title, date, and short label. Later, the same framework can expand into deeper artifact records and cross-links.
This keeps the kiosk polished now without forcing the full database or content architecture to be finished up front.

gallery.title: Historic Photos
gallery.intro: A simple image gallery is one of the fastest ways to make the kiosk feel substantial and share what's in your archive.

media.title: Watch & Listen
media.intro: This page can support a single opening-day video, a narration track, or a short oral history and still feel intentional.

items.title: Exhibit Introduction
items.text: Replace this with an embedded MP4, Vimeo, YouTube, or local exhibit intro video.

items.title: Oral History Clip
items.text: Add an audio player, speaker name, and short excerpt to create a stronger personal connection.

media.footerNote: Tip: one strong media item is enough for launch. You do not need a huge library for this to feel real.

about.title: Steelworks Center of the West
paragraphs: The Steelworks Center of the West preserves and shares the historic archives, artifacts, and buildings of the Colorado Fuel and Iron Company and related activities that shaped the industrialization of the American West.
This kiosk preview is designed as a focused opening-day experience that can expand into a larger interactive system over time.
Use this section for institutional context, exhibit orientation, sponsor acknowledgment, or visitor prompts.
